members.name: InitArgs
members.signature: keyword_struct InitArgs { ... }
members.comment: Manages initialization arguments for Zig's guidance system, owns state, ensures invariant correctness.

members.name: parse
members.signature: fn parse(args: []const []const u8) -> error{MissingValue}!InitArgs

members.name: cmdInit
members.signature: fn cmdInit(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Initializes the sync engine with allocator and command arguments, preparing for Zig command execution.

members.name: gitDiff
members.signature: fn gitDiff(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, cwd: []const u8, staged: bool) -> []u8

members.name: splitDiffByFile
members.signature: fn splitDiffByFile(diff: []const u8, out: *std.ArrayList([]const u8), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) -> void
members.comment: Split a unified diff into per-file chunks starting at each "diff --git" header.

members.name: chunkFilePath
members.signature: fn chunkFilePath(chunk: []const u8) -> []const u8
members.comment: Extract the file path from "diff --git a/<path> b/<path>".

members.name: chunkIsExplainGenJson
members.signature: fn chunkIsExplainGenJson(chunk: []const u8, guidance_dir: []const u8) -> bool
members.comment:
Return true for auto-generated guidance JSON files (not real code changes).
Uses guidance_dir (e.g. ".guidance") to identify which paths to ignore.

members.name: parseHunkRanges
members.signature: fn parseHunkRanges(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, chunk: []const u8) -> [][2]u32
members.comment: Parse `@@ -X,Y +A,B @@` hunk headers; returns owned [start, end) pairs in new-file coords.

members.name: CommitMemberInfo
members.signature: keyword_struct CommitMemberInfo { ... }
members.comment: Snapshot of a member's name, line, comment, and signature at the point a commit is prepared.

members.name: deinit
members.signature: fn deinit(self: CommitMemberInfo, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) -> void

members.name: memberLineNum
members.signature: fn memberLineNum(obj: std.json.ObjectMap) -> ?u32
members.comment: Extract a ?u32 line number from a JSON member object value (.line field).

members.name: lineInRanges
members.signature: fn lineInRanges(line: u32, hunk_ranges: []const [2]u32, context: u32) -> bool
members.comment: Return true when `line` falls within any hunk range expanded by `context`.

members.name: appendMemberIfInRange
members.signature: fn appendMemberIfInRange(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, member: std.json.Value, hunk_ranges: []const [2]u32, context: u32, out: *std.ArrayList(CommitMemberInfo)) -> void
members.comment:
Append a CommitMemberInfo to `out` when the JSON member object is valid and
its line falls within `hunk_ranges` (or when hunk_ranges is empty).

members.name: loadChangedMembers
members.signature: fn loadChangedMembers(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, guidance_root: []const u8, rel_path: []const u8, hunk_ranges: []const [2]u32) -> []CommitMemberInfo
members.comment: Load guidance JSON for rel_path and return members whose lines overlap hunk_ranges.

members.name: generateCommitMessage
members.signature: fn generateCommitMessage(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, diff: []const u8, changed_files: []const []const u8, guidance_dir: []const u8, guidance_root: []const u8, api_url: []const u8, model: []const u8, debug: bool) -> []u8

members.name: writeTmpCommitMsg
members.signature: fn writeTmpCommitMsg(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, msg: []const u8) -> []u8

members.name: cmdCommit
members.signature: fn cmdCommit(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Handles command execution with allocator and arguments, returning void.

members.name: loadCommitModelFromConfig
members.signature: fn loadCommitModelFromConfig(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, cwd: []const u8) -> []const u8
members.comment:
Read `models.commit` or `models.default` from guidance-config.json.
Returns an owned slice; caller must free. Returns error when absent.

members.name: GenArgs
members.signature: keyword_struct GenArgs { ... }
members.comment: Manages generation parameters for sync engine; owns configuration struct; ensures consistent initialization.

members.name: parse
members.signature: fn parse(args: []const []const u8) -> error{MissingValue}!GenArgs
members.comment:
Parse gen subcommand arguments. Returns error.MissingValue when a
flag-with-value is the last argument (fail fast; do not silently drop).

members.name: ResolvedGenPaths
members.signature: keyword_struct ResolvedGenPaths { ... }
members.comment: Resolved absolute paths for a gen run. All fields are owned; call deinit().

members.name: deinit
members.signature: fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) -> void

members.name: resolveGenPaths
members.signature: fn resolveGenPaths(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, ga: GenArgs, cwd: []const u8) -> ResolvedGenPaths
members.comment:
Resolve workspace, json_dir, and db_path (→ .guidance.db) to absolute
paths.  db_path is the SQLite vector database; it defaults to the value
in guidance-config.json, or DEFAULT_GUIDANCE_DB_PATH if not set.

members.name: setupCspEnhancer
members.signature: fn setupCspEnhancer(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, ga: GenArgs, cfg: *const config_mod.ProjectConfig, csp: *comment_sync_mod.CommentSyncProcessor) -> void
members.comment:
Wire up the LLM enhancer on a `CommentSyncProcessor`.

Allocates an `Enhancer` on the heap (required because `CommentSyncProcessor`
holds a `?*Enhancer` pointer).  The returned pointer must be freed with
`teardownCspEnhancer` after the processor is done.

members.name: teardownCspEnhancer
members.signature: fn teardownCspEnhancer(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, csp: *comment_sync_mod.CommentSyncProcessor) -> void
members.comment: Release the heap-allocated enhancer previously set by `setupCspEnhancer`.

members.name: setupEnhancer
members.signature: fn setupEnhancer(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, ga: GenArgs, cfg: *const config_mod.ProjectConfig, processor: *sync_mod.SyncProcessor) -> void
members.comment:
Wire up the LLM enhancer for automatic comment generation.
Model selection: fast slot (if set) > default slot.
Uses resolveLlmConfigForThinking to ensure thinking models use /api/chat endpoint.
Logs a warning and leaves processor.enhancer null if initialisation fails.

members.name: processFiles
members.signature: fn processFiles(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, processor: *sync_mod.SyncProcessor, ga: GenArgs, paths: ResolvedGenPaths) -> usize
members.comment:
Dispatch to single-file, explicit-scan, or full-workspace processing.
Fails fast and propagates the first error encountered.
Returns the count of source files processed.

members.name: guidanceDbIsUpToDate
members.signature: fn guidanceDbIsUpToDate(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, db_path: []const u8, json_dir: []const u8, capabilities_dir: []const u8) -> bool
members.comment:
Returns true when `db_path` is newer than every dependency that `syncGuidanceDb`
consumes:
  • every .json file under `json_dir/src/`
  • `json_dir/semantic-aliases.json`
  • `json_dir/capability-mapping.json`
  • `json_dir/guidance-config.json`
  • every file under `capabilities_dir` (when non-null)

Returns false (→ sync needed) when the db is absent or any dep is newer.

members.name: syncGuidanceDb
members.signature: fn syncGuidanceDb(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, json_dir: []const u8, guidance_db_path: []const u8, cfg: *const config_mod.ProjectConfig, verbose: bool) -> void
members.comment:
Sync .guidance.db (SQLite vector database with in-process cosine similarity).
Creates an embedding provider from config and calls vector_db.syncDatabase.
Failures are logged as warnings but do not abort the gen pipeline.

members.name: clearSynthesisCacheAt
members.signature: fn clearSynthesisCacheAt(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, db_path: []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
Open the guidance db at `db_path` and delete all llm_cache entries.
Best-effort: failures are silently ignored.

members.name: generateSemanticAliases
members.signature: fn generateSemanticAliases(guidance_dir: []const u8, verbose: bool) -> void
members.comment:
Extract keywords from all guidance JSON files, rank by frequency, and generate
optimized semantic aliases using LLM consolidation.
NOTE: This preserves existing semantic-aliases.json if it exists.

members.name: cmdGen
members.signature: fn cmdGen(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Generates a Zig command string using provided allocator and arguments.

members.name: cmdGenImpl
members.signature: fn cmdGenImpl(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, ga: GenArgs) -> void
members.comment:
Core gen implementation shared by `gen` and `check`.

Pipeline (per source file):
  incremental check → test (once/language) → lint → fmt → guidance → touch JSON

Incremental detection is always active: a file is skipped when its guidance
JSON is at least as new as the source.  Pass `ga.force = true` to override.
The guidance JSON is always touched after successful processing so its mtime
acts as the universal "all phases passed" marker across all languages.

members.name: validateAllJsonSchema
members.signature: fn validateAllJsonSchema(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, json_dir: []const u8, verbose: bool) -> void
members.comment:
Walk `json_dir/src/` and validate every .json file against the GuidanceDoc schema.
Prints a warning for each invalid file but does not abort the gen run.

members.name: cmdStatus
members.signature: fn cmdStatus(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Validates and processes Zig command arguments, returning a status update.

members.name: cmdClean
members.signature: fn cmdClean(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Cleans up memory by allocating and returning a cleaned Zig allocation.

members.name: guidanceJsonPath
members.signature: fn guidanceJsonPath(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, workspace: []const u8, json_dir: []const u8, src_abs: []const u8) -> []const u8
members.comment: Extracts a JSON path from a Zig source file using an allocator and workspace parameters.

members.name: cmdMapCapabilities
members.signature: fn cmdMapCapabilities(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
Map CAPABILITY.md files to source files by analysing AST JSON content.
Updates .guidance/capability-mapping.json with fresh keyword and source mappings.
Preserves the "mapping" (file→capability) section; regenerates "capability_keywords".

Usage: guidance map-capabilities [--guidance-dir DIR] [--workspace DIR] [--dry-run]

members.name: isCapabilityKeywordToken
members.signature: fn isCapabilityKeywordToken(tok: []const u8) -> bool
members.comment:
Return true if `tok` is a plausible Zig/Python identifier keyword.
Accepts: camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, dotted.paths, but not
punctuation-heavy strings or all-lowercase common words.

members.name: runCommand
members.signature: fn runCommand(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, argv: []const []const u8) -> bool
members.comment:
Run an arbitrary command (full argv, no template substitution).
Returns true on exit code 0.

members.name: runPhaseCommand
members.signature: fn runPhaseCommand(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, argv_template: []const []const u8, file_path: []const u8) -> bool
members.comment:
Substitute `{file}` tokens in `argv_template` with `file_path`, then run
the resulting command via `runCommand`.

Returns true on exit code 0, false otherwise.

members.name: collectFilesWithExts
members.signature: fn collectFilesWithExts(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, dir_abs: []const u8, exts: []const []const u8) -> [][]const u8
members.comment:
Walk `dir_abs` recursively and collect all files whose extension matches
any member of `exts` (e.g. `.{".zig"}`).
Returns an owned slice of owned absolute paths; caller must free each and
then the slice.

members.name: runBuiltinFilePipeline
members.signature: fn runBuiltinFilePipeline(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, cfg: *const config_mod.ProjectConfig, processor: *sync_mod.SyncProcessor, src_abs: []const u8, ga: GenArgs) -> bool
members.comment:
Run the per-file pipeline for one built-in source file:
  1. lint  (if configured and not skip_lint)
  2. fmt   (if configured and not skip_fmt)
  3. guidance  (AST → JSON via SyncProcessor)
  4. touch JSON  (always — makes JSON mtime the universal "all phases passed" marker)

Formatting runs AFTER lint so that:
  - Semantic lint errors are caught before any file modification.
  - The formatter normalises whitespace before the AST is parsed, ensuring
    line numbers in the JSON are stable and accurate.

Returns false when lint fails (caller should abort the batch).

members.name: runBuiltinLanguagePipeline
members.signature: fn runBuiltinLanguagePipeline(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, cfg: *const config_mod.ProjectConfig, processor: *sync_mod.SyncProcessor, language: []const u8, stale_files: []const []const u8, all_files: []const []const u8, guidance_root: []const u8, ga: GenArgs) -> void
members.comment:
Run the full built-in pipeline over a set of source files that share the
same language group:
  1. Test suite (once, if any file is stale relative to the test marker)
  2. Per-file: lint → fmt → guidance → touch JSON

`language` is a short tag (e.g. "zig") used for the test marker path.
`stale_files` are the files that need processing (source newer than JSON).
`all_files` are ALL source files for this language (used for test marker check).
`guidance_root` is the absolute path to the guidance directory (e.g. .guidance).

members.name: cmdSyncComments
members.signature: fn cmdSyncComments(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Processes a Zig source file to extract and return sync-comment data.

members.name: cmdMigrateComments
members.signature: fn cmdMigrateComments(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment: Processes and migrates comment data during sync operations.

members.name: cmdScrub
members.signature: fn cmdScrub(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
Walk .guidance/src/**/*.json and blank any synthetic comments in-place.
Usage: guidance scrub [--guidance-dir DIR] [--dry-run]

members.name: scrubCount
members.signature: fn scrubCount(value: *std.json.Value) -> usize
members.comment: Count and scrub synthetic comments in a Value tree; returns count blanked.

members.name: cmdTodo
members.signature: fn cmdTodo(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
Dispatch to todo subcommands.
Usage: guidance todo <new|triage|checklist|status|list|abandon> [args...]

members.name: cmdDiary
members.signature: fn cmdDiary(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
Append a timestamped entry to the current work item's DIARY.md.
Usage: guidance diary "<message>"

members.name: cmdCheck
members.signature: fn cmdCheck(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, args: []const []const u8) -> void
members.comment:
`guidance check` runs the complete RALPH loop:
  test → lint → fmt → guidance (all languages) → structure → db

It is the recommended entry point for pre-commit hooks and CI.
Incremental detection is always active: only stale files are processed.

members.name: parseHunkRangesPub
members.signature: fn parseHunkRangesPub(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, chunk: []const u8) -> [][2]u32
members.comment: Converts a null-terminated string into a slice of u32 values representing hunk ranges.

members.name: loadChangedMembersPub
members.signature: fn loadChangedMembersPub(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, guidance_root: []const u8, rel_path: []const u8, hunk_ranges: []const [2]u32) -> []CommitMemberInfo
members.comment: Loads changed member information from a Zig file using allocator, guidance root, relative paths, and hunk ranges.

members.name: chunkIsIgnoredPub
members.signature: fn chunkIsIgnoredPub(chunk: []const u8, guidance_dir: []const u8) -> bool
members.comment: Checks if a chunk should be ignored based on guidance data.

members.name: chunkFilePathPub
members.signature: fn chunkFilePathPub(chunk: []const u8) -> []const u8
members.comment: Converts a chunk of bytes into a public file path string.

members.name: splitDiffByFilePub
members.signature: fn splitDiffByFilePub(diff: []const u8, out: *std.ArrayList([]const u8), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) -> void
members.comment: Splits a diff array by file pub key, returning an allocated slice of bytes.
